divergences: Go templates render {{.Project}}, {{.Dataset}}, {{.Prefix}} into SQL; no schema validation at boundary
substituteIntervalVars and SubstituteTestVars do raw string replacement of @start/@end/@test_start/@test_end -- no parameterization
prependDropForReplace mutates SQL with regex before execution
language_boundaries.confidence_reason: Go template rendering + raw string substitution of SQL with no schema contract; SQL file contents are hand-written and not validated against BQ schema at build time

divergences: Connection config serialized as JSON env var GRANICUS_DEST_CONNECTION / GRANICUS_SOURCE_CONNECTION -- Python must parse same JSON shape
GRANICUS_REFS env var passes dependency references as JSON map -- Python scripts must know to read it
GRANICUS_METADATA_PATH is a shared file path -- Python writes JSON, Go reads it back; no schema enforced
Credentials passed inside connection JSON -- crosses process boundary in env vars
language_boundaries.confidence_reason: Implicit env-var contract between Go and Python with no shared type definitions; metadata file exchange uses untyped JSON

divergences: InlineSQL path allows SQL embedded in YAML config -- no file to audit
language_boundaries.confidence_reason: Same Go-template-to-SQL bridge as boundary_001; inline SQL adds another unvalidated input path

divergences: Delegates entirely to PythonRunner -- same implicit env var contract
dlt scripts expected to write rows_loaded/tables_created/load_duration to metadata file but this is not enforced
language_boundaries.confidence_reason: Same implicit subprocess bridge as boundary_002; dlt metadata format is a convention not a contract

divergences: Shell scripts receive no structured input beyond working directory and OS env
No metadata exchange mechanism -- scripts are fire-and-forget with exit code
language_boundaries.confidence_reason: Minimal contract: Go only checks exit code; shell scripts have full OS access
Fixed query against BigQuery system table; stable schema; no user-controlled input

language_boundaries.to_function: Cloud Run Job container (same Go binary, different process)
divergences: Env vars and Pub/Sub ResultEnvelope are the contract between orchestrator and worker
Result read via Pub/Sub subscription with message attributes for routing
Worker image version may differ from orchestrator -- no version pinning
language_boundaries.confidence_reason: Cross-process boundary with implicit Pub/Sub message contract; worker image version not locked to orchestrator

language_boundaries.to_function: BigQuery metadata queries (row counts, null counts)
language_boundaries.confidence_reason: Dynamic SQL generated by Go for BQ metadata introspection; stable pattern

flags.reason: Confidence 0.55: implicit Go-to-Python boundary via env vars and subprocess. No shared type definitions, no schema for metadata file exchange.
flags.suggested_fix: Define a shared JSON schema (e.g., granicus_env_contract.json) that both Go and Python validate against. Add schema validation in PythonRunner before launch and in Python SDK on read.

flags.reason: Confidence 0.55: delegates to PythonRunner with same implicit contract. dlt metadata keys are a convention.
flags.suggested_fix: Same as PythonRunner.Run fix. Additionally, document required metadata keys for dlt scripts and validate on read.

flags.reason: Confidence 0.60: shell scripts receive no structured contract. Full OS access with no sandboxing.
flags.suggested_fix: Consider adding a minimal contract for shell runners (expected env vars, exit code semantics). Document which shell scripts exist and what they do.

flags.reason: Confidence 0.65: Go template rendering into SQL with raw string substitution. No schema validation at boundary.
flags.suggested_fix: Add a dry-run validation step that parses rendered SQL before execution. Consider parameterized queries instead of string substitution for @start/@end.

flags.reason: Confidence 0.65: same template boundary as SQLRunner plus inline SQL from config.
flags.suggested_fix: Validate inline SQL at config load time. Apply same fixes as SQLRunner.

flags.reason: Confidence 0.60: cross-process Pub/Sub contract with no version pinning between orchestrator and worker image.
flags.suggested_fix: Add a version field to ResultEnvelope. Pin worker image digest in dispatch config. Add timeout and dead-letter handling.

flags.reason: Cannot statically determine which Python scripts exist or what env vars they read. The contract is entirely implicit.
flags.suggested_fix: Create a Python-side SDK (granicus_sdk) that reads and validates all GRANICUS_* env vars with typed accessors, making the contract explicit.

flags.reason: Dynamic dispatch via Runner interface. Concrete implementations (SQLRunner, PythonRunner, DLTRunner, ShellRunner, GCSRunner, S3Runner) are registered at runtime by cmd.buildRegistry. Static analysis cannot follow these calls.
flags.suggested_fix: Document the runner type -> implementation mapping. Consider a compile-time registration table instead of runtime map.

flags.reason: Interface-based dispatch to RunnerDispatch.Execute. Concrete implementations (LocalDispatch, CloudRunJobDispatch) are selected at runtime via GRANICUS_RUNNER_DISPATCH env var.
flags.suggested_fix: Document the dispatch mode -> implementation mapping. Add exhaustive dispatch mode validation.

flags.reason: Dynamic dispatch via StateBackend interface. Selects SQLite or Firestore backend based on GRANICUS_STATE_BACKEND env var at runtime.
flags.suggested_fix: Document valid backend values and their implications. Add exhaustive validation.

flags.reason: Dynamic dispatch via PipelineSource interface. Selects local filesystem or GCS+Firestore source based on GRANICUS_PIPELINE_SOURCE env var.
flags.suggested_fix: Document valid source values and their implications.

flags.reason: Go init() function in internal/server/metrics.go registers Prometheus metrics collectors. This executes implicitly on package import, creating a hidden DAG edge from any importer of the server package to Prometheus registration.
flags.suggested_fix: Replace init() with an explicit InitMetrics() function called from server startup path.

flags.reason: Public trust_level HTTP endpoint that triggers pipeline execution reaching BigQuery (sensitive data). Auth is optional -- if no API keys configured, auth is disabled entirely (auth.go line 26).
flags.suggested_fix: Make auth required when running in production. Add a config validation warning when api_keys is empty in non-dev environments.

flags.reason: Public trust_level CLI entry point that reaches BigQuery with credentials. No authorization beyond OS-level access control.
flags.suggested_fix: Document that CLI access is gated by OS user permissions and credential file access. No action needed if deployment model is trusted.

flags.reason: Creates BigQuery client using credential file path from connection config. No audit logging of credential access.
flags.suggested_fix: Add structured log line when credentials are loaded, including which connection and credential path (but not the credential content).

flags.reason: Serializes connection config including credential paths into env vars passed to Python subprocesses. Credentials cross process boundary without audit.
flags.suggested_fix: Log when credentials are passed to subprocesses. Consider using a credential proxy instead of passing paths.

flags.reason: Reads GRANICUS_PUBSUB_PROJECT, GRANICUS_FIRESTORE_PROJECT, GRANICUS_EVENTS_TOPIC env vars and creates Pub/Sub client with application default credentials. No audit logging of credential access.
flags.suggested_fix: Add structured log line documenting which project and topic are being used for event publishing.

flags.reason: Pub/Sub entry point that receives events from external sources and writes to local filesystem. Malformed messages are acked without dead-letter handling.
flags.suggested_fix: Consider nacking malformed messages or routing to a dead-letter topic. Add rate limiting.

flags.reason: Health endpoint (/api/v1/health) is exempt from auth. If no keys are configured, all endpoints are unauthenticated. Constant-time comparison is correctly used for key validation.
flags.suggested_fix: Add a warning log when auth is disabled. Consider making auth mandatory in production.
